concerns.text: FR-001 requires ingesting 'raw FASTQ files' and processing them to ASVs. T013a only describes 'fetching' datasets (potentially processed tables via load_dataset) and T013c is a separate denoising task. There is no explicit task linking the ingestion of RAW FASTQs to the DADA2/QIIME2 pipeline, creating a gap where the system might ingest processed tables instead of raw reads, violating FR-001.

FR-003 mandates 'exact permutation tests' or '≥9999 permutations' if sample size < 20. T018 only specifies '≥999 permutations' generally. There is no task to implement the conditional logic to detect small sample sizes and switch to exact/high-permutation modes, leaving a gap in statistical validity for small strata.

FR-009 requires subsampling if 'projected memory usage > 6 GB'. T021 triggers on dataset size > 6GB (static) and T038 is a reporting task. There is no task to implement the 'projection' logic (estimating RAM usage before loading) to determine *when* to trigger subsampling, risking OOM crashes before the check occurs.

SC-003 requires measuring the 'standard deviation of the rank index' and checking if it is ≤ 0.5. T029 only tasks 'determining top driver... and comparing rankings'. There is no task to calculate the standard deviation or generate a pass/fail result against the 0.5 threshold, making the success criterion unverifiable.

Edge Cases (Dataset Availability) mandates a specific fatal error JSON format: `{"level": "FATAL", "msg": "No sufficient ITS datasets found: <count> valid datasets, minimum required"}`. T040 vaguely says 'implement fatal error handling' without specifying the required JSON structure or exact message content, risking a violation of the spec's logging requirements.

T039 tasks generating a 'power_analysis_report.md' and implementing power analysis logic. The spec (FR-005 Edge Cases) only mentions a 'minimum practical stratum size' as a justification for skipping small strata; there is no explicit requirement to perform a full power analysis or generate a dedicated report. This appears to be scope creep not covered by the spec.
